title: deutzia × rosea f. multiflora
intro: Deutzia × rosea f. multiflora, a flowering plant species within the Hydrangeaceae family, is a cross between Deutzia scabra and Deutzia crenata, known for its clusters of small, fragrant white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Deutzia × rosea f. multiflora include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. Deutzia × rosea f. multiflora can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its flowers in hot water, or by using its leaves and flowers in a tincture or infusion for medicinal purposes.
